transcript: Yeah, this is Dustin at DHL… Uhh… [exhales]And, uh, we got some cargo here that was attempted to be delivered. No one answered the door.
No, you don’t have to, you just open up the white metal door and just put it inside.
Well, this one’s from Guam; it’s a little bit different. Uh—
Uh, cabling from Guam and it’s C.O.D. and, uh, we need ya up here—front and center—with an I.D.
Uh, they’re gonna have to do somethin’.
''[groans] [delay]Okay, yeah, that’s the alarm. We gotta get this resolved tonight! [exhales][exhales] [delay]
What is that loud noise in the background?
We’re on the dock, sir, by the water.
Alright, and— and it’s C.O.D? I didn’t buy anything.
Alright, well send it back. Thanks a lot.
We’ll be out there in ten minutes, fifteen minutes.
Well no, you’re gonna get no money; that’s it.
We want a check cut or a PayPal payment. [exhales] [delay]
But you’re not gonna get nothing.
Well, sir, you haven’t even looked at the goods inside.
[groans] [delay, reverb]What’s all that noise up ‘ere?[groans] [delay, reverb]
[belches] [delay, pitch-shifted]Yeah, this is, uh, Dustin at DHL shipping. What appears to be the trouble up here? [belches] [delay, pitch-shifted]
I have no idea. I have— Uh, you told me that you had some stuff that, uh, we’re supposed to get—
[in background] I have ordered! It’s a Christmas gift! Hold on a second.
Alright, hold on a minute. This is my wife.
[in background to Dick] ’Kay, thanks, hang up.—
Now what in the heck’s goin’ on up, uh, here?! [pitch-down]
I have no idea. I’ve been gone shopping.
Dick, what’s going on? You need to tell me.
Okay, I got ya, I see. I got ya. [pitch-down]I see. I understand.—[pitch-up]
[in background to Lana] I have no idea. He said something about a big thing from Guam—somewhere. It has nothin’ to do with you, I don’t think.
[in background to Lana] I think. I wanna talk to the [unclear].
Yeah, but, uh…[to LPC ]'' Where’s this coming from?
[to Lana] Did you order somethin’ from Guam?
From Guam?! Hold on.[to LPC ] Hello?
Yeah, this is Dustin'' at DHL calling.
I need to see some I.D. tonight!
Okay, I don’t know what you’re talking about, my husband doesn’t know what you’re talking about.—
Is there—? What are you talking about? My husband is a professional musician. He is Dick Dale, King of the Surf Guitar. And we don’t have any idea what you’re talking about.
Well this stuff has come a long way!—by boat!—d’you understand? [pitch-down]

We don’t understand this! First of all, I thought you were talking about Christmas stuff that I just recently ordered—little things! I didn’t know you were talking about wires from Guam! And I don’t know who would’ve ordered it, because Dick is a professional! I mean, he— he wouldn’t order something and tell you that he didn’t order it! I mean, that would be—
—a hell of a thing to do to somebody, like that.So, I don’t understand what’s going on. I just got home!
It’s a scam? Oh, okay. Alright.—
I wanna talk to the [unclear]. That has nothing to do with me—
Well, I think I can read my own handwriting, sir. I can see what’s on the screen.
It says, uh, “Detailed description o’ contents”. This came from Guam by boat. I need a check. I’ve been out there twice. Ya know, quit horsin’ around! Tell ya— I don’t know what ta tell ya!—
Don’t tell me a— about horsing around, pal! Okay?—
[in background] [unclear] We don’t even know what he’s talking about!
So don’t give me that crap.
I didn’t order anything from Guam. I got no money— Uh, I bought nothing from Guam. I have no cases or cables, so don’t say I’m messing around, okay?
Goddamn—! No, you get it from someplace else, okay?
Lemme talk— Lemme talk to your wife—
That’s it. Don’t call me anymore!
I need to talk to your wife.
